repo_prior_art.cite: none; API panel catalogue covers Q1 basis/sign/spectral thresholds, and panel_honest_did_phase covers Honest-DiD support-function aggregation, not active pre-period EIF rank

published_prior_art.cite: ChenSantAnnaXie2025 derives closed-form EIFs and overidentification restrictions; BJS2024 derives robust efficient imputation; neither states the four-estimator rank-zero equality frontier
ChenSantAnnaXie2025 supplies the closed-form EIF weights; the displayed equality is the definitional projection residual once q_EIF and q_e are assumed
generic Weyl/Davis-Kahan singular-value perturbation; new only as applied to H_pre,e

repo_prior_art.cite: none; q1_spectral_phase_transition/p1_markov is an event-window row-space visibility threshold, not a BJS-SA-CS-Wooldridge EIF equality frontier

published_prior_art.cite: BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024, SunAbraham2021, CallawaySantAnna2021, Wooldridge2021, RothSantAnna2023Efficient, and ChenSantAnnaXie2025 do not give this necessary-and-sufficient named-estimator IF equality condition

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The rank boundary only uses the pre-period projection residual H_pre,e after fixing b_a with e0=CS; it does not account for possible estimator-specific canonical/post-period score-loading differences among SA, CS, Wooldridge, and BJS, so rank H_pre,e=0 need not by itself imply full influence-function equality.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The named four-estimator implication inherits the same missing residual term: unless Section 6 adds an assumption or diagnostic forcing the non-preperiod score loadings to coincide, equality of the three H_pre,e ranks is not a well-posed iff classifier for identical full influence functions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add estimator-specific canonical score loadings b_a^e and either prove/assume b_a^e=b_a^BJS for the compared target or extend the diagnostic to include the residual d_e=b_a^BJS-b_a^e alongside H_pre,e.
In Section 9 add a small T=3 or T=4 matrix example where H_pre,SA is zero but H_pre,CS is nonzero, with all non-preperiod loadings shown equal; this would make the boundary visibly non-tautological.

State explicitly which published Wooldridge/ETWFE implementation induces the comparison weights w^W_gtc and whether those weights are restricted to not-yet/never-treated cohorts or can be signed residual-regression weights.

recommended_next_step: Revise Section 6 and both conjectures to include estimator-specific canonical/post-score residuals, then rerun novelty review on the repaired iff statement.
